description: Bootstrap the Rust workspace for treb-cli-rs: establish foundry dependency chain, async runtime, error handling strategy, CLI skeleton, and CI pipeline.

userStories.title: Cargo Workspace and Toolchain Configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a properly configured Cargo workspace with two initial crates (treb-cli binary and treb-core library) and all toolchain/linting configuration files so that subsequent phases have a solid foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo build succeeds from workspace root
cargo run -p treb-cli prints output and exits 0
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings
rust-toolchain.toml specifies a nightly toolchain compatible with edition 2024
All config files (rustfmt.toml, clippy.toml, .gitignore) exist at workspace root
cargo check --workspace passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: Foundry Crate Integration and Alloy Re-exports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want foundry-config and foundry-common as git dependencies with alloy primitive re-exports in treb-core so that downstream crates use foundry-aligned types without version conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo check --workspace succeeds with foundry crates resolving
treb_core::primitives::Address, treb_core::primitives::B256, and treb_core::primitives::U256 are importable and usable
A simple test in treb-core constructs an Address::ZERO and asserts it is zero
foundry_config::Config::load() is callable (import compiles)
The specific foundry git commit is documented in Cargo.toml
cargo clippy --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Patched svm-rs-builds to v0.5.24 to fix duplicate SOLC_VERSION_0_8_31 build error

userStories.title: Error Strategy and Async Runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a project-wide error handling pattern using thiserror in treb-core and anyhow in treb-cli with a tokio async runtime so that all future code follows consistent error and async patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: treb_core::error::TrebError is a public enum with Config, Registry, Forge, and Io variants
TrebError implements std::error::Error and Display via thiserror
treb_core::Result<T> is a public type alias using TrebError
std::io::Error converts into TrebError via From
treb-cli main function is async and returns anyhow::Result<()>
A unit test in treb-core creates a TrebError::Config and formats it
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo check --workspace passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: CLI Skeleton with Clap
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clap-based CLI with version/help output and stub subcommands for all planned treb commands so that the command structure is established for future implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo run -p treb-cli -- --help prints help with all subcommands listed
cargo run -p treb-cli -- --version prints version string
cargo run -p treb-cli -- run prints 'not yet implemented' message
cargo run -p treb-cli -- list and cargo run -p treb-cli -- ls both work (alias)
All planned subcommands are present in the Commands enum: Run, List, Show, Init, Config, Verify, Tag, Register, Sync, Version, Networks, GenDeploy, Compose, Prune, Reset, Migrate, Fork, Dev
cargo clippy --workspace passes
cargo check --workspace passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI and README
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a CI pipeline and placeholder README with build instructions so that every push is validated and new contributors can get started quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and is valid YAML
CI workflow defines jobs for check, test, clippy, and fmt
CI workflow uses Swatinem/rust-cache for faster builds
README.md exists with build, run, and test instructions
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo check --workspace passes (typecheck)
